brief_document.title: Server visibility endpoints and integration test
brief_document.purpose: Expose visibility queries via gRPC and HTTP endpoints in aion-server, and provide an end-to-end integration test proving workflows can be found by custom search attributes.
brief_document.task: Add visibility query endpoints to aion-server: ListWorkflows (filtered) and CountWorkflows. Write an integration test that starts a workflow, sets custom search attributes from the engine, and verifies the workflow can be found by attribute value through the visibility store.

requirements.title: gRPC visibility query endpoints
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL add gRPC service methods: ListWorkflows (takes filter, returns Vec<WorkflowSummary>) and CountWorkflows (takes filter, returns count).
acceptance: Proto definitions exist for ListWorkflows and CountWorkflows.
Server handlers delegate to the VisibilityStore.
Filter fields are mappable from proto to ListWorkflowsFilter.

requirements.title: HTTP visibility query endpoints
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL add HTTP endpoints: GET /workflows (list with query params for filters) and GET /workflows/count (count with query params).
acceptance: GET /workflows returns a JSON array of WorkflowSummary.
Query params filter by type, status, time range, and custom attributes.
Pagination via limit and offset query params.

requirements.title: Integration test: search by custom attributes
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL have an integration test that: starts a workflow, records SearchAttributesUpdated with a customer_id attribute, and verifies list_workflows with a customer_id filter returns the workflow.
acceptance: Workflow with customer_id=12345 appears in list_workflows filtered by customer_id=12345.
Workflow does NOT appear when filtered by customer_id=99999.
Filtering by workflow_type + customer_id narrows results correctly.
count_workflows returns 1 for the matching filter.

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement dashboard UI — that belongs in the dashboard cluster.
SHALL NOT implement WebSocket streaming for visibility updates.
SHALL NOT implement an Elasticsearch backend — libSQL is the default.

constraints.text: VisibilityStore is a trait in aion-store, not coupled to any specific backend.
The libSQL implementation must support filtering by all standard fields and custom attribute predicates without full table scans.
Search attribute types are registered per namespace; type mismatches are errors, not silent coercions.
Visibility updates must not block the EventStore append path in server mode.
WorkflowSummary must include enough data for dashboard rendering without per-workflow history reads.
The search attribute event (SearchAttributesUpdated) is a recorded event subject to replay and determinism — it must be replayable.

resolved_context.intention: Once an engine runs more than a handful of workflows, operators need to find them: show me all failed order-processing workflows from the last hour, find the workflow for customer X, list all running workflows of type Y. Without a query layer, the only access path is by WorkflowId — which requires knowing the ID up front. This cluster adds search attributes (user-defined key-value metadata on workflows) and a visibility index that supports filtered listing, full-text-like search on attributes, and time-range queries. This is the operational backbone that makes dashboards, alerting, and debugging possible at scale.

provenance.quote: Once an engine runs more than a handful of workflows, operators need to find them: show me all failed order-processing workflows from the last hour, find the workflow for customer X, list all running
